zh.lifeSummary: 當John Brittan Webber於1567年5月23日出生在英國戴文巴恩斯塔普爾時，他的父親Sir William John Henry of Griffith Webber III35歲，母親Alice Peake35歲。他於1587年11月6日與Elizabeth Davie在英國戴文普萊姆斯普萊姆斯 聖 安德魯結婚。他們至少是6個兒子的父母。他去世於1647年10月17日在他的家鄉享年80歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Brittan Webber于1567年5月23日出生在英国戴文巴恩斯塔普爾时，他的父亲Sir William John Henry of Griffith Webber III35岁，母亲Alice Peake35岁。他于1587年11月6日与Elizabeth Davie在英国戴文普萊姆斯普萊姆斯 聖 安德魯结婚。他们至少是6个儿子的父母。他去世于1647年10月17日在他的家乡享年80岁。

nameMeanings.name: Webber
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Devon and Somerset): occupational name for a weaver, from Middle English webber, an agent derivative of Webb .
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Weber   ‘weaver’.

variants.name: Webb
meanings: English: occupational name for a weaver, from early Middle English webbe (Old English webba (masculine) or webbe (feminine), probably used of both male and female weavers). This word survived into Middle English long enough to give rise to the surname, but was already obsolescent as an agent noun; hence the secondary forms with the agent suffixes -er and -ster (see Webster , Webber and compare Weaver ).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish (Ashkenazic) surnames, cognates of 1, including Weber and Weberman.
History: Richard Webb, a Lowland Scot, was an admitted freeman of Boston in 1632, and in 1635 was one of the first settlers of Hartford, CT.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: Brittan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mainly North American: modern coinage, taken from the traditionally Celtic-speaking region of north-west France, known in medieval Latin as Britannia, because it was settled by refugees from Cornwall and Devon following the establishment of the Anglo-Saxon kingdom of Wessex. Its adoption as a given name has also been influenced by Britt , of which it is sometimes regarded as the full form. In recent years it has rapidly established itself as a popular name in the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When John Brittan Webber was born on 23 May 1567, in Barnstaple, Devon, England, his father, Sir William John Henry of Griffith Webber III, was 35 and his mother, Alice Peake, was 35. He married Elizabeth Davie on 6 November 1587, in Plymouth St Andrew, Plymouth, Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons. He died on 17 October 1647, in his hometown, at the age of 80.

ko.lifeSummary: John Brittan Webber(이)가 1567년 5월 23일 영국 데폰 바른스타플에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William John Henry of Griffith Webber III(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Peake(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1587년 11월 6일 영국 데폰 플림우스 플림우스 스트 앤드루에서 Elizabeth Davie(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1647년 10월 17일 그의 고향에서 80세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Davie em 6 de novembro de 1587, em Plymouth St Andrew, Plymouth, Devon, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Brittan Webber родился 23 мая 1567 г. в местности барнстапл, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William John Henry of Griffith Webber III было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Peake, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Davie 6 ноября 1587 г. в местности плимоут ст андрю, плимоут, девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей. Он умер 17 октября 1647 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 80 лет.
